steps.title: Subtract equations to find x
steps.content:
Subtracting the second equation from the first eliminates $y$:
$$\left(\frac{5}{4}x + 2y\right) - \left(\frac{3}{4}x + 2y\right) = 17 - 15$$
$$\frac{2}{4}x = 2$$
$$x = 4$$

steps.title: Find y and compute the target value
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 4$ into the second equation:
$$\frac{3}{4}(4) + 2y = 15$$
$$3 + 2y = 15$$
$$2y = 12$$
$$y = 6$$

Evaluate the target expression:
$$\frac{11}{4}(4) + 6(6) = 11 + 36 = 47$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
